title: arabis bellidifolia subsp. subcoriacea
intro: Arabis bellidifolia subsp. subcoriacea, also known as the Alpine Rockcress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Arabis bellidifolia subsp. subcoriacea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can be consumed fresh or dried to harness its medicinal properties.
